title: Event-Driven Service Pattern
description: Enforces event-driven architecture with sealed event hierarchy, event-carried state transfer, and partitioned topic ordering. Events are facts in past tense published from handlers through ports.

book-references: Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 4–5 — Event Notification & Event-Carried State Transfer
Head First Design Patterns Ch. 2 — Observer Pattern

nodes.name: Event Producer
nodes.description: Application handler that publishes domain events after successful state changes. Events are published through the EventPublisher port, never directly to Kafka.

nodes.name: Message Broker
nodes.description: Kafka broker with partitioned topics. Guarantees per-partition ordering via employerId partition key.

nodes.name: Event Consumer
nodes.description: Downstream consumer that reacts to domain events. Decoupled from producer — no shared compile-time dependency on handler classes.

relationships.description: KafkaEventPublisher sends events to partitioned topics with employerId as partition key
Consumers receive events from Kafka topics with at-least-once delivery semantics

constraints.description: Event types must be named in past tense (Created, Confirmed, Rejected). Never imperative (CreateEndorsement, ShouldConfirm).
All event types must be records in the sealed EndorsementEvent interface. Compile-time exhaustiveness enforced.
Events must carry sufficient contextual data for consumers. No callback queries to source database.
Events are published from application/handler/ classes only. Infrastructure adapters must never publish events autonomously.
All events partitioned by employerId for per-employer ordering. Never change partition key strategy without migration planning.
Kafka producer configured with acks=all. All in-sync replicas must acknowledge. Do not weaken for throughput.
